texto: I don't mean to step on anyone's toes, and I don't mean to seem arrogant, but if you want to be foreverly remembered as anything great you have to live your life alone, hated, ignored, and character assassinated, and if you believe this to be true than you truely are a hater.
explicacao: Orientação direta de B.L. Kennison sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “I don't mean to step on anyone's toes, and I don't mean to seem arrogant, but if you want to be f…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, philosophy-of-life, anyone, útil em momentos de reflexao.

frase_original: I don't mean to step on anyone's toes, and I don't mean to seem arrogant, but if you want to be foreverly remembered as anything great you have to live your life alone, hated, ignored, and character assassinated, and if you believe this to be true than you truely are a hater.

texto: The world is like a giant Alcoholic whining day to day about what others are doing. Instead of of living harmoniously and individually for Good Orderly Direction. If we have faith in our God, than why should we care how others are living their lives?
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a B.L. Kennison: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “The world is like a giant Alcoholic whining day to day about what others are doing. Instead of of…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que vida, philosophy-of-life, reflexao pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.
